A sophomore at the University of San Francisco studying Data Science and UX/UI, with a focus on design that is expressive, culturally grounded, and technically considered. An international student whose background shapes every project.

Adobe Illustrator · Character Design

India
Mascot Design

A mascot created for India in Adobe Illustrator as part of a branding project focused on inclusive design and cultural representation. The brief asked us to move beyond stereotypes and think critically about how identity gets communicated through design. I drew on Indian cultural symbols and worked in vector to build a character that felt both specific and celebratory.

Adobe Illustrator Character Design Branding Cultural Identity

Adobe Illustrator · Vector Art

USF Campus Architecture

For this project I photographed buildings around USF's campus, then traced each photo into vector shapes in Adobe Illustrator using only five colors. The assignment was inspired by Josef and Annie Albers' abstract work, and the goal was to reduce architecture down to its essential shapes, shadows, and structure. The nine pieces work both as individual compositions and as a unified 24x24 inch grid.

Photoshop · Photography

Progress is Becoming

Clean Slate / Progress / Earned

This triptych started with something I was already making: a painting of Krishna. I used that process as the subject itself — the blank start, the frustrating middle, and finally holding the finished thing. Shot on a DSLR and edited in Photoshop, each panel has its own color palette and composition while the three work together as one story.

Photoshop
Design Fundamentals, USF

Canva · Social Media Design

Café International

Instagram event graphics I designed for the Intercultural Center at USF during my time as International Community Intern. Each post was built around a different theme, so I switched up the visual style every time to match the mood of the event.
